Functions for creating, initializing, and managing ActionableDialog objects in a UI framework.

# Purpose
This C header file defines the interface for managing `ActionableDialog` objects within an application. It includes function declarations for creating, initializing, and manipulating `ActionableDialog` instances. The functions allow users to create a new dialog, initialize it with a name, retrieve its internal `Dialog` object, and manage its action bar type and click configuration. Additionally, the file provides functions to push and pop `ActionableDialog` objects onto and from a `WindowStack`, facilitating their integration into a user interface. The file includes necessary headers for graphics and UI components, ensuring that the `ActionableDialog` can interact with other parts of the application.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `actionable_dialog_private.h`
- `applib/graphics/gtypes.h`
- `applib/ui/action_bar_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/click.h`
- `applib/ui/dialogs/dialog.h`
- `applib/ui/window_stack.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### actionable\_dialog\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:actionable_dialog_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L30>)

Creates a new ActionableDialog on the heap.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new instance of an `ActionableDialog`. It allocates memory for the dialog and initializes it with the specified debug name. Ensure that the returned pointer is checked for null to handle memory allocation failures. This function must be called before using the dialog in any operations.
- **Inputs**:
    - `dialog_name`: A string representing the debug name for the `ActionableDialog`. It must be a valid, null-terminated string. The caller retains ownership of the string, and it must not be null.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a newly created `ActionableDialog`. If memory allocation fails, it returns null.
- **See Also**: [`actionable_dialog_create`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#actionable_dialog_create>)  (Implementation)


---
### actionable\_dialog\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:actionable_dialog_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L36>)

Initializes an ActionableDialog structure.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize an `ActionableDialog` structure before using it. This function sets up the dialog with the specified name and prepares it for use by configuring its window handlers. Ensure that the `actionable_dialog` pointer is valid and not null before calling this function. This function does not allocate memory for the `ActionableDialog`; it only initializes the provided structure.
- **Inputs**:
    - `actionable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ActionableDialog` structure to initialize. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership and is responsible for ensuring the structure is valid.
    - `dialog_name`: A string representing the debug name to assign to the `ActionableDialog`. The string must be null-terminated. The caller retains ownership of the string.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`actionable_dialog_init`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#actionable_dialog_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### actionable\_dialog\_get\_dialog<!-- {{#callable_declaration:actionable_dialog_get_dialog}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L41>)

Retrieves the internal Dialog object from an ActionableDialog.
- **Description**: Use this function to access the internal `Dialog` object contained within an `ActionableDialog`. This is useful when you need to interact with or modify the `Dialog` directly. Ensure that the `ActionableDialog` pointer is valid and not null before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `actionable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ActionableDialog` from which to retrieve the internal `Dialog`. Must not be null. Caller retains ownership.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the internal `Dialog` object of the specified `ActionableDialog`.
- **See Also**: [`actionable_dialog_get_dialog`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#actionable_dialog_get_dialog>)  (Implementation)


---
### actionable\_dialog\_set\_action\_bar\_type<!-- {{#callable_declaration:actionable_dialog_set_action_bar_type}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L51>)

Sets the type of action bar for an actionable dialog.
- **Description**: Use this function to specify the type of action bar for an `ActionableDialog`. If the action bar type is `DialogActionBarCustom`, you must provide a valid `ActionBarLayer` pointer. For other types, the `action_bar` parameter is ignored, and the action bar is set to `NULL`. This function must be called after the `ActionableDialog` is initialized.
- **Inputs**:
    - `actionable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ActionableDialog` whose action bar type you want to set. Must not be null.
    - `action_bar_type`: Specifies the type of action bar to use. Can be one of the predefined types or `DialogActionBarCustom`.
    - `action_bar`: Pointer to an `ActionBarLayer` to assign to the dialog. Required only if `action_bar_type` is `DialogActionBarCustom`. Must not be null in this case.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`actionable_dialog_set_action_bar_type`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#actionable_dialog_set_action_bar_type>)  (Implementation)


---
### actionable\_dialog\_set\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable_declaration:actionable_dialog_set_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L60>)

Sets the ClickConfigProvider for an ActionableDialog.
- **Description**: Use this function to assign a ClickConfigProvider to an ActionableDialog. This is necessary for configuring how the dialog responds to click events. If the dialog uses a custom action bar, this function will have no effect, as the custom action bar manages its own click configuration. Ensure that the ActionableDialog is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `actionable_dialog`: Pointer to an ActionableDialog. Must not be null. The function sets the ClickConfigProvider for this dialog.
    - `click_config_provider`: The ClickConfigProvider to set for the ActionableDialog. This parameter defines how click events are handled.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`actionable_dialog_set_click_config_provider`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#actionable_dialog_set_click_config_provider>)  (Implementation)


---
### actionable\_dialog\_push<!-- {{#callable_declaration:actionable_dialog_push}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L67>)

Pushes an `ActionableDialog` onto a `WindowStack`.
- **Description**: Use this function to display an `ActionableDialog` by adding it to a specified `WindowStack`. Ensure that the `ActionableDialog` is properly initialized before calling this function. This function does not handle null pointers, so both parameters must be valid and non-null. It is typically used in applications where dialog management is required within a window stack.
- **Inputs**:
    - `actionable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ActionableDialog` to push onto the window stack. Must not be null and should be initialized before use.
    - `window_stack`: Pointer to a `WindowStack` where the `ActionableDialog` will be pushed. Must not be null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`actionable_dialog_push`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#actionable_dialog_push>)  (Implementation)


---
### app\_actionable\_dialog\_push<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_actionable_dialog_push}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L71>)

Pushes an `ActionableDialog` onto the application's window stack.
- **Description**: Use this function to display an `ActionableDialog` by adding it to the application's window stack. This function is a wrapper that simplifies the process of pushing an `ActionableDialog` onto the stack, making it visible to the user. Ensure that the `ActionableDialog` is properly initialized before calling this function. This function does not handle null pointers, so the `actionable_dialog` parameter must not be null.
- **Inputs**:
    - `actionable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ActionableDialog` that you want to push onto the window stack. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the `ActionableDialog`.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`app_actionable_dialog_push`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#app_actionable_dialog_push>)  (Implementation)


---
### actionable\_dialog\_pop<!-- {{#callable_declaration:actionable_dialog_pop}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/dialogs/actionable_dialog.h#L75>)

Removes an `ActionableDialog` from the window stack.
- **Description**: Use this function to remove an `ActionableDialog` from the window stack it was previously pushed onto. This is typically done when the dialog is no longer needed or when transitioning to a different dialog or screen. Ensure that the `ActionableDialog` has been pushed onto a window stack before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `actionable_dialog`: Pointer to an `ActionableDialog` to remove from the window stack. Must not be null. The function does not check for null pointers, so passing a null pointer will result in undefined behavior.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`actionable_dialog_pop`](<actionable_dialog.c.md#actionable_dialog_pop>)  (Implementation)
